title: Religious Statistics - Christian Religions that grow and shrink in America

transcript.text: Okay, what's up? Religious trends in America. Let's see who is, which religions are growing, which religions are shrinking. I think Islam is growing big time in America. But we'll find out. So this is a Gallup poll. I think surveying 2,000 Americans in 2003.

And so now we're comparing 1992 to 2001. And we've got Catholic, Southern Baptist, other Baptist, Methodist, Presbyterian, Episcopal, Lutheran, Pentecostal, other Protestant, Mormon, Eastern Orthodox, Jewish, other specific religions, none, non-religious, and then undesignated. Okay?

So let's start with Catholic. Catholic. 26, 26, 24, 27. Actually, I'll do it by year. 92, 26, 93, 26. This is a percentage of the world, right? Of America. So one quarter of Americans, as of 1992, 26% were Catholic. Okay?
So 92, 26, 93, 26, 94, 24. 95, 27. Ooh. 96, 25. 97, 26. 98, 27. 99, 28. 2000, 27. 2001, 25. So they kind of hovered about the same for the last 10 years. Up until 8 years ago, huh?
Okay. Okay, so Southern Baptist. Okay, let's see. 92, 9. 93, 10. 94, 10. 95, 10. 96, 8. 97, 8. 98, 8. 99, 8. 2008. 2001, 6. So Southern Baptist kind of going down a little bit.
But, yeah. But kind of holding steady, but going down. Other Baptists.
